_comment: Zenodo deposition metadata. Upload via the Zenodo REST API (POST /api/deposit/depositions with this under {"metadata": ...}) or paste fields into the web form. Only ORCIDs and the related preprint DOI remain as placeholders. The Zenodo record holds: the DOI (citation), the small reference-DB indexes (AMRFinderPlus/CARD/VFDB/PlasmidFinder, each well under 50 GB), and this MANIFEST + README pointing to the large indexes on S3.

metadata.title: Dragon Indexes for AllTheBacteria and bacterial reference databases
metadata.description:
Prebuilt, cloud-native (Zarr) sequence-search indexes produced by Dragon, enabling sub-second alignment of query sequences against millions of bacterial genomes and curated AMR/virulence/plasmid gene catalogues.

The large genome-collection indexes (AllTheBacteria — 2,292,989 assemblies, 8.1 TB; GTDB; E. coli; K. pneumoniae; S. aureus; Shigella; Salmonella enterica; Salmonella Typhi) are hosted on AWS S3 (s3://dragon-zarr, region eu-west-2) via the AWS Open Data Program because they exceed repository size limits (projected total ~12 TB). This Zenodo record provides the citable DOI, the small reference-database indexes (AMRFinderPlus, CARD, VFDB, PlasmidFinder), and the manifest/README with S3 URIs, sizes, checksums, and usage.

Format: Zarr (chunked) — tools stream only the chunks needed, so a single-gene query never downloads the full index.

Note on CARD: the CARD database has restricted redistribution; the CARD-derived index here ships derived metadata only / may be omitted — obtain CARD from card.mcmaster.ca.

related_identifiers._comment: AWS Open Data registry entry (large indexes)

metadata.notes: Large indexes (>50 GB) are not uploaded to Zenodo; they live in s3://dragon-zarr (AWS Open Data Program). See MANIFEST.md for the full catalogue, byte sizes, object counts, and SHA256 checksums. Managed by London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM).
